name: Baby Routh's Rosemary Muffins with Goat Chees
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, simmer milk, raisins, and chopped rosemary for 2 minutes.
2. Remove from heat, add butter, and stir until melted. Let cool.
3. In a large bowl, mix dry ingredients: flour, sugar, baking powder, salt.
4. Beat egg into the cooled milk mixture.
5. Add to the dry ingredients and mix lightly just until the dry ingredients are moistened.
6. Spoon 1/3 of the batter into 12 greased muffin cups.
7. Place 2 tablespoons (2 tsp) of goat cheese in the center of each batter portion.
8. Cover the cheese with the remaining batter, dividing among all the muffins.
9. Bake for approximately 20 minutes in a preheated 350°F oven, or until brown and springy in the center.
10. Serve muffins hot or cool. If desired, a 3/4-inch cube of cream cheese may be substituted for goat cheese. Without cheese, muffins are still delicious!
